title: Item created through form doesn't show creator in creation log

posts.author: tamirma (Tamir Maoz)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

I created a new form to submit requests to my team.
The only issue with this form is that the Creation Log shows me as the creator os all requests, no matter who submitted the form, even if the submitter is a team member (who is subscribed to the workspace).
I use the Creation Log field to send notification emails about item’s status changes, so having the correct requester in this column is crucial.

Please advise.

Thanks!

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi… How they are uploading the form?
Vía desktop app, web app ir mobile app?

posts.author: tamirma (Tamir Maoz)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,
I created the form in the web app.

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml: No, question Is, what platform users are using for filling the form

posts.author: tamirma (Tamir Maoz)
posts.cookedHtml:
To the best of my knowledge they are using the web client too.
BTW anonymous form is unchecked.

posts.author: Adrian (Adrian F. Guzman)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m having the same issue. I have the item automatically assigned the task to me based on the form answers. All tasks are showing as created by me regardless of who is assigned to the task.

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
Im having this kind of issue from the last year, in my case the users complete the form via a smartphone with android, so a link sended via whatsapp is opened via the default web browser installed (normally its chrome), the forms are submitted and enter to my board with ME as the creator when this is not real.

What i have searched and found is that (in my use case) our users doesnt have logged in Monday.com via chrome web browser, because they are working directly via the Monday.com android native App.

So, as users are not logged in monday.com on the same browser that its used to fill and send the forms, this forms returns without real user info, because they cant be retrieved, so it puts me as the creator.

Thats why i was asking from what platform users are uploading forms, because if the platform are not the same where monday.com user are logged in, then there is no way to get that info.

There is a previous feature request on:

Auto-add user records when submitting a form Feature requests
Well, yes and no. The creation log is helpful, but the creation log entry is not reference-able for notifications or automations. So at the moment, one of my staff has to populate an actual “people” column by hand, based on the person in the creation log.

posts.author: kswiss50 (Kendall Shortt)
posts.cookedHtml:
I ran into the same issue with the only way around it would be to create a dropdown list to have them select their name in the form.

You can’t run automatons on that however so doesn’t really solve the core issue.

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello @brett-monday.com / @Julia-monday.com… who Is the right person in Monday to talk with about this issues?

posts.author: Elisa (Elisa Hutchison)
posts.cookedHtml: We also utilize lots of forms. Since I am the administrator for our company and set everything up my name is associated with all of these tasks and updates. I would prefer to have the person left blank so we know that it came from a form rather than associating my account with everything.

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Elisa, that will depend on the company workflow / use cases.
In my case i build up a form so every field technician can submit a form reporting some key items on the vehicle inspection previous to his use. Its important to know each user that is using the vehicle fleet.
Thats why i need to know who are submitting this forms.

posts.author: Elisa (Elisa Hutchison)
posts.cookedHtml: @hlopezvc I agree. I always put in a Requested By field so the person entering the information is required to put a name that we can refer back to. However, a creation log or update added with the form shows my name. I’d like to get away from that since it really wasn’t me.

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, besides this anyone could accidentally / intentionally send a form with another´s name.

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi guys,

Forms for Internal Use - Auto Assign on Form/item Creation using Status Field Feature requests
Excellent. Do you think this will be rolled out in the next couple weeks or more like a few months? Just trying to figure out what I may need to do in the mean time. Thank you for any insight that you may have.

Would the responses in this thread be of any help? Are you referring to having forms submitted by people who are actually members of your account?

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Julia-monday.com how are you?

Responding to your question: yes, we are referring to members/guests accounts of our accounts. But the thread talks abouts and automation between the creator log column and a person column. What we are saying is that the forms are not carrying the user info to the creator log column in user/guest of our accounts, so Monday.com assigns by default the board creator as the creation log user, and that´s its not real, and its useless in case of the automation you have mentioned.

posts.author: tamirma (Tamir Maoz)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Julia-monday.com
Yes, the form is submitted by people in our account and even subscribed to the same workspace.
The result is always the same, the Creation Log shows me as the item creator, and that makes using this column for automation impossible.
I’m assuming that the issue is related to the fact that I created the form, but I expect the real user to be registered as the item creator.

posts.author: MadProx (Damian Castillo)
posts.cookedHtml:
We experience some issues when using automation’s. The person who creates automation’s on a board is the person shown as the owner regardless if you have a column to assign it to another member.

May not be your issue but if you have automation’s running, check to see who the creator is and you may find that this is the default owner ever time.

posts.author: brett-monday.com (Brett Dalton)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi All!

Brett here stepping in for Julia, sorry for the delay here. Our forms should show the creator of the form within the creation log, just as long as the submitter is in fact logged into the account.

Do any of you experience the opposite of this behavior when you know for a fact a user is actively logged in?

Thanks!

posts.author: Johnathon (Johnathon Pasley)
posts.cookedHtml: @brett-monday.com , as a suggestion, is it possible to autofill in a Name / Text field if a user is logged in or conditionally hide the field if a user is filled in . Just as thought.

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Brett!
Our field technicians are logged in the Monday.comApp, not in the web browser App, so every creator log comes to my board with my name in it.
Could you develop any solution for Android app users?
Like an internal application to complete forms in the cases of users / guests? That could solve the problem in any case.

posts.author: tamirma (Tamir Maoz)
posts.cookedHtml: @brett-monday.com hi Brett, our users are logged in (in fact I didn’t allow anonymous submission is the form settings, and the item is still registers as if I created it.

posts.author: Jody (Jody Poland)
posts.cookedHtml: I ran into this also. It’s cumbersome and affected our other automation, but if you don’t have any other automation on this board it might work for you. Use the status column and create a label for each team member. Then, create an automation based on the "name label that appears in the status field. The automation would change it to a people column in the board. It worked well until we added a second automation to the board.

posts.author: jake.armstrong (Jake Armstrong)
posts.cookedHtml:
@brett-monday.com

Hey,

I’ve also ran into this problem. I have just done a test with our Customer Care team and their requests are still showing as me being the creator. We also have too many staff members to create a status for each one, we’d need to use a dropdown method but then cannot use an automation to assign someone who is not subscribed to the board. Ideally, we don’t want the whole company subscribed.

posts.author: Coksi (Teodor Coporda)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @tamirma,

I have found a workaround for myself while managing forms and answers from them.
You can allow your Guests and Members to choose from “Status” column their name and then through automations, you can set “If status is something, assign someone as person in People column”. It works well without problem, but I understand why you want the functionality of adding the person who answered as the creator (logically).

Also sorry for late answering, I have only found this few moments ago, hope it helps you and someone else.

posts.author: jake.armstrong (Jake Armstrong)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @brett-monday.com,

Has this been looked into yet? It’s still an issue that we keep running into.

Thanks,

posts.author: elig (Eli)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello, same issue here. Commenting to track resolution.

posts.author: n.renard (Nic Renard)
posts.cookedHtml: Same issue here! Is this planned to be resolved anytime soon?

posts.author: ClaireM-HD (Claire Miller)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @brett-monday.com. I’m having the same issue - do you have an update? I had a team member who has a monday.com account submit a form and it shows me as the creator.

posts.author: Topdownjohnny (John)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @brett-monday.com,

I think the problem is that forms can be filled out without being logged in (through shared link and even when anonymous submission is turned OFF), in that case the creator of the form is assigned. If the user is logged in, then the user is assigned with this automation.

I think a lot of problems would be resolved if when designing the form there is an option to disallow filling out the form when the user is not logged in. In that case the user should first login.

John

posts.author: celestew (Celeste Wickard)
posts.cookedHtml: Has there been any update on this or how to fix this where it can show the actual user that submitted the form? It is showing that I submitted the form and any additional updates when in face it is another account member that is doing so.

posts.author: Rhyno3000 (Ryan)
posts.cookedHtml:
G’day All

Yep this problem still exists and is a bit of pain.

Any one in our company with a Monday account that submits the form we have created always assigns myself as the creator - not the actual user who has filled in the form.

Happy to share the form and links if required to review this issue.

Regards

posts.author: bjornery (Bjorn Christianson)
posts.cookedHtml: This is a pain point for my team. We created a help desk board and now that we’ve deployed, people are submitting requests and I don’t know who it is, which makes it hard to help them. I don’t want to have to add new lines for name and email, then I would have to duplicate effort on every ticket to correctly set the user. Please fix ASAP.

posts.author: bmeyers05 (BRANDON MEYERS)
posts.cookedHtml: We are also struggling with this in a similar way. We have started a board for PTO tracking with a basic form entry but also allow users (while logged in or not), they are not members of the PTO board, only management and HR. We currently allow a comment on the bottom (which is an update) and if they type something in there it always looks like it is from me as the board owner. This is not great when tracking PTO as the record needs to look as if it is coming from the employee or generic and just an anonymous entry. I tried the check box option for Anonymous Submissions but it did not change anything and I still show up at the creator. Would really like to see a change on this as it affects another board as well in a different way where it looks like that board owner for a service ticket form for our clients always types the update even though it comes from the form (client). This shouldn’t be that hard that if someone types in the update field on form entry only that it shows up an an anonymous form entry. I’ve gone so far as thinking I should create an email in our O365 called FormEntry@mycompany.com or anonymous@mycompany.com and making that the owner on all boards to get this to work but that is ridiculous and uses an open license for nothing.

posts.author: mmeneses (Matt Meneses)
posts.cookedHtml:
Replying to this to track progress as well. We are trying to eliminate a step of manually setting our “requestor” in our Purchasing requests board to a text field from the form. We use a form for anyone in our office to request a purchase of item(s). It would be so nice to not have to manually assign every person for each request.

The challenge with the solution some provided using the Status column + automation combination is that it doesn’t scale very well when you have more than 40 users using that same form. If you can make drop-downs work for automations that would give people another workaround.

posts.author: ShawnBaden (shawn baden)
posts.cookedHtml: This really is a pain! The form doesn’t even offer the option for entering the requestor manually - the person column doesn’t even show up on the form.

posts.author: Rhyno3000 (Ryan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Monday.com team

Any update here please?
This has been going on for a year almost !

Regards

posts.author: hdurey (Hunt Durey)
posts.cookedHtml: @brett-monday.com can you please update this post with Monday’s plan to address these needs? For us, the best solution would be to add people columns in forms with the ability to look up and select one / multiple account users on the form. This doesn’t solve the creator / automation issue for creators who are not signed in, but it’s the next best option IMO.

posts.author: BrunoM (Bruno Melo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Ok, the this is what I know about this, and how you could solve the problem, most times the person is submiting the form via cellphone, and since we have downloaded an app, we assume that the form will know who filled it, but that is not the case, our forms are filled in a browser app, like chrome or safari, so we need to log into monday in theses apps, but these webpages will always tell us to use an app.

But we can enter in the browser, use the computer version and go to the login* page, and it will allow us to login with the browser, and just like that, your problem is solved

*You may need the uninstall the app for that to work, but you can install it later.

posts.author: mw01 (MW)
posts.cookedHtml: Any update on this Monday.com?

posts.author: Christophe (SC)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

The situation is the same for me:

  • the form was created by myself in the relevant board, and when another user completes the form and submits it, the automate
    image932×106 5.25 KB

    won’t assign the person who as submitted the form but myself.

The work around I have so far found is to tell the persons who are supposed to use the form, to duplicate the original form and use their own copy: with this solution, they will by default be assigned as the Creator of the new entry. BUT this solution is not clean, it multiplies uselessly the number of forms.

I support all the above requests to modify this

Thanks in advance and cheers for the nice tool so far.

posts.author: ormatt1 (Matt)
posts.cookedHtml:
Right, so I’ve got a form created for users in our company to submit issues they find on our website. All works fine, but I get added as the creator of the items that people add via the form. Is there a way for me to set the creator to another user? (cannot be solved by adding another column for that user since I still get added as the creator, which is not what I want).

If there’s no way to change the creator of an item, then would the only way to do it be to log in using the user I wish to set as the creator and recreate all of the automations using their account instead?

posts.author: ajwhitman (AJ W)
posts.cookedHtml:

You might want to commiserate with the people in this thread: Item created through form doesn't show creator in creation log

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @ormatt1 and @ajwhitman!

The creator will only show up in the creation log if the following conditions are met:

  1. The user needs to have access to that specific board (either main board or subscribed to the private/shareable board)
  2. The user needs to have an active session
  3. The user needs to be logged into this account only and not another

Here’s an example of how an active session works:

I load up Chrome. I login to monday.com. Now I have an “Active Session” in Chrome and only Chrome.

You can tell you have an active session because if you open a new tab, and visit whatever.monday.com, you’re already logged in. If you don’t have an active session, then it will require you to enter your user name and password when loading a new tab and visiting whatever.monday.com. Since my active session is only in Chrome, this means if I open up Safari and go to whatever.monday.com, I do not have an active session in Safari. It will make me login again.

The desktop app is, for all intents and purposes, a web browser that only can access monday.com. So regardless of whether you’re logged in there, it will have zero desirable impact on the creation log showing who submitted the form. This is because you can’t fill out a form on the desktop app. Users will need to have that active session in the same browser that they’re filling the form out in.

The reason the system can’t recognize the form creator without this active session is the following:
The active session is a web browser cookie. That cookie contains various bits of info, one of which is a sort of User ID for the person who has the active session. So it knows that it’s Talia who has an active session on whatever.monday.com. The creation log will look at that cookie, and be able to confidently presume “hey, it’s safe to presume that anybody submitting a monday.com form through this specific web browser is Talia, as she’s the only one logged in to monday.com in this browser”.

Let me know if you have any questions! This probably won’t get changed because we can’t change the way cookies work, but I hope this explanation helps.

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
@TRB-monday.com hi…
Most of users that are requesting this feature want to use forms with internal collaborators that are inside monday (users, guests).

Why dont to develop some feature like Internal forms (could be some inside Monday new tool) that could be recognizing the user directly from the app/web platform/android iOS ?

posts.author: dmenaker (Dave Menaker)
posts.cookedHtml: My issue is somewhat related, someone earlier did mention this. As the account admin, I created all the forms and automations and everything. I now have someone else listed as the board owner, but every time a new item is created from a form submission (from an external user) I am listed as the item owner. I feel this should be the board owner, not the form creator. I’m going crazy with all the notifications I don’t need to see.

posts.author: Jaewoo (Jaewoo Kim)
posts.cookedHtml:
The “active session” language is overly complicated. What I found with to solve this issue is to simply make sure you have one tab open that points to monday.com (logged in) & another that points to the form.

So whoever is attempting to submit through the form. They will need to have this setup for the creation log to work properly.

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml: Some of these frustrations can be addressed through the best practice use of an “Automation Bot” account, if you are just trying to avoid getting “credited” as the Creator on form submissions. Create an admin account using a general email for your organization (info@, clientcare@, etc.). It needs to be an email you can validate so you can activate the account. Then use that account as the company admin to set up forms. We also recommend using this as the authenticated account for Integromat, Zapier, most automations, etc. It does take up a paid seat but it clearly separates automated activity like form submissions from human users who don’t want to be subscribed on the items.

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
But that doesnt solve the issue. Just assigns a non human account to be the ítem creator.
Problem Will be still there

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml: True @hlopezvc - it won’t solve the issue by inserting a regular human user as the Creator, but it will avoid having an admin become the Creator by default and thus getting notifications and updates they don’t care about. It’s not a complete solution but for many of our clients it solves a big pain point.
